macro_segments.title: Introduction to Daf 68 and the Final Laws of the Four Executions (Arba Mitot)

micro_segments.title: Overview of the daf and the topic of witchcraft (kishuf) according to Rabbi Akiva

macro_segments.title: The Question of Rabbi Akiva's Source: Learning from Rabbi Eliezer or Rabbi Yehoshua

micro_segments.title: The Gemara's challenge to the Mishnah: Did Rabbi Akiva learn this from Rabbi Yehoshua?

macro_segments.title: Introduction to Daf 68 and the Final Laws of the Four Executions (Arba Mitot) (continued)

micro_segments.title: Clarification of Rabbi Akiva's ruling on witchcraft: illusion versus actual deed

macro_segments.title: The Question of Rabbi Akiva's Source: Learning from Rabbi Eliezer or Rabbi Yehoshua (continued)

micro_segments.title: Introduction to Rabbi Eliezer and his historical context, including the Tanur shel Achnai story

macro_segments.title: The Deathbed Scene of Rabbi Eliezer: A Profound Narrative on Torah, Isolation, and Knowledge

micro_segments.title: The arrival of Rabbi Akiva and his colleagues to visit the dying Rabbi Eliezer; the confrontation with his son Hurcanus over the removal of tefillin before Shabbat
Rabbi Eliezer's harsh prediction about the fates of his students, particularly Rabbi Akiva
Rabbi Eliezer's lament: the comparison of his arms to rolled-up Torah scrolls and his vast untransmitted knowledge
The teaching about three hundred (or three thousand) halachot on planting gourds; the only student who asked—Rabbi Akiva
The encounter between Rabbi Eliezer and Rabbi Akiva on the way: the magical acts of planting and uprooting gourds through witchcraft
The sages' questions to Rabbi Eliezer about leather vessels and receptacles; his consistent positions
Rabbi Eliezer's final ruling on the purity of the shoe and the departure of his soul in purity
Rabbi Yehoshua's declaration ending the excommunication (nidduy) and Rabbi Akiva's eulogy

macro_segments.title: Resolution of the Source Question and the Permissibility of Learning Witchcraft for Educational Purposes

micro_segments.title: The Gemara's conclusion: Rabbi Akiva learned from Rabbi Eliezer but did not understand; he later learned and comprehended from Rabbi Yehoshua
The Gemara's question and answer on how Rabbi Eliezer could actually perform witchcraft: permission for learning to understand and teach, not to do

macro_segments.title: Transition to Ben Sorer u-Moreh (The Rebellious and Wayward Son)

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the new category of stoning: the ben sorer u-moreh and the principle of judging based on future conduct (al shem sofo)

macro_segments.title: The Age Requirements for Ben Sorer u-Moreh: Between Manhood and Adulthood

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah's criteria: from two pubic hairs (age 13) until the beard fills out; clarification of euphemistic language
The Gemara's derivation from Scripture: why the categories must be 'son' and not 'man'
Rav Yehuda's interpretation: a son who is close to the vigor of a man, marking the narrow window of culpability

macro_segments.title: Can a Minor Father a Child? The Debate Between Rav Chisda and Rava

micro_segments.title: Rav Chisda's ruling: if a minor fathers a child, that child cannot become ben sorer u-moreh; derivation from the phrase 'a man has a son'
The Gemara's analysis of the verse's phrasing: how the word order teaches both that the father must be an adult and that he must have just become one
Rava's position: a minor cannot father a child, derived from the law of property return to a convert (gezel ha-ger)
The Gemara's explanation of why a minor necessarily has no children: no investigation is needed because minors cannot father children

topical_tags.term: Al Shem Sofo (Judged by Future Conduct)
